name: Mail
docs: A minimal email app. Threads live in the authority plane; surfaces may only touch them through the declared capabilities. Thread.state is one of inbox | archived | snoozed. awaitingReply=true means the last message in the thread is from the other party and the user has not responded.

eventDate.description: Set when the email is about a schedulable event

category.description: Heuristic category: work | legal | recruiting | club | class | career | promo

input.filter: optional: inbox | archived | snoozed | awaiting_reply | all

capabilities.docs: List the user's threads. Default filter is all.
Move a thread out of the inbox. Inverse: mail.unarchive.
Return an archived thread to the inbox.
Hide a thread until the given time, then it returns to the inbox.
Reply to a thread. External side effect: requires a fresh user gesture; the authority plane rejects programmatic calls.

policies.docs: Surfaces run under CSP default-src 'none' in a sandboxed iframe. The only I/O is the capability bridge. Generated code containing fetch/XHR/WebSocket/import() is rejected by the gate.
A surface must declare the capabilities it uses in its header; the bridge refuses any call outside the declared set.
Capabilities marked gesture:true (mail.send) are rejected by the authority plane unless invoked within 1500ms of a real user gesture.

conformance.docs: The surface must render and make its first successful capability call within 8 seconds, with zero uncaught JS errors.
